```{=html}
<div style="width:88%;margin:0 auto;background-image:radial-gradient(circle,#E0DBE5 0.6px,transparent 0.6px);background-size:18px 18px;padding:1.5em 1em;border-radius:18px;">

 <div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:1.1fr 1fr;gap:1.2em;align-items:center;">

  <div style="background:#1E1E2E;border-radius:14px;padding:0.85em 1em 1em;font-family:'Maple Mono',monospace;font-size:0.62em;line-height:1.95;text-align:left;color:#F8F7F0;box-shadow:0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.18);">
   <div style="display:flex;gap:0.35em;margin-bottom:0.7em;">
    <span style="width:0.5em;height:0.5em;border-radius:50%;background:#FF5F56;"></span>
    <span style="width:0.5em;height:0.5em;border-radius:50%;background:#FFBD2E;"></span>
    <span style="width:0.5em;height:0.5em;border-radius:50%;background:#27C93F;"></span>
   </div>
   <div><span style="color:#69B4F0;font-weight:700;">ph-html/</span> <span style="color:#5C6B80;">← a directory</span></div>
   <div><span style="color:#5C6B80;">└── </span><span style="color:#FFB84D;">SKILL.md</span> <span style="color:#5C6B80;">← the only requirement</span></div>
  </div>

  <div style="text-align:left;">
   <div style="font-family:'Maple Mono',monospace;font-size:0.5em;color:#9B8EA0;letter-spacing:0.12em;text-transform:uppercase;margin-bottom:0.5em;">at minimum</div>
   <div style="font-size:0.78em;color:#2C3E50;line-height:1.5;margin-bottom:0.75em;">
    A folder bearing the skill's name, holding a single <code style="font-family:'Maple Mono',monospace;color:#C4881A;background:#FEF5E7;padding:0.1em 0.4em;border-radius:5px;">SKILL.md</code>.
   </div>
   <div style="background:#FFFFFF;border-radius:10px;padding:0.6em 0.85em;font-size:0.55em;color:#564232;line-height:1.5;box-shadow:0 4px 14px rgba(86,84,162,0.1);border-left:3px solid #2C8475;">
    My <code style="font-family:'Maple Mono',monospace;color:#2C8475;">/ph-html</code> has only this one file, yet it styles every HTML component across my blog and slides.
   </div>
  </div>

 </div>

</div>
```
